content.text:
1	# Delivery Acceptance and Project Architecture Audit (Static-Only)
2	
3	## 1. Verdict
4	- Overall conclusion: Partial Pass
5	
6	Reason: The repository is substantial and largely aligned to the clinic workflow prompt, but there are material gaps and mismatches, including one security-control miss on login anti-replay and several requirement-to-implementation/API contract gaps that prevent a full pass.
7	
8	## 2. Scope and Static Verification Boundary
9	- Reviewed:
10	  - Documentation and run/test/config instructions: README, API/design docs.
11	  - Flask entry points, app factory, middleware, route registration, core route modules.
12	  - Core models and utilities for auth/RBAC, anti-replay, idempotency, scheduling, visits, coverage, reminders, encryption, logging/audit.
13	  - Test suite structure and representative unit/integration/security tests (including e2e presence statically).
14	- Not reviewed:
15	  - Runtime behavior under real browser timing, scheduler drift in production, network/TLS trust chain behavior, Docker orchestration health behavior.
16	- Intentionally not executed:
17	  - Project startup, Docker, tests, or any external services (per instruction).
18	- Manual verification required:
19	  - End-to-end UI behavior and visual quality consistency under real browser interaction.
20	  - Real concurrency behavior for race/conflict paths in multi-user runtime.
21	  - Real operational performance and slow-query fidelity versus request-latency proxying.
22	
23	## 3. Repository / Requirement Mapping Summary
24	- Prompt core goal mapped: internal/LAN-only clinic operations platform with account/auth, demographics, assessments with explainable risk, scheduling/holds/conflicts, visit state machine, coverage zones, reminders, local observability, and security/privacy controls.
25	- Main implementation areas mapped:
26	  - Auth/security/middleware: app/routes/auth.py, app/utils/antireplay.py, app/utils/middleware.py, app/config.py.
27	  - Core business flows: app/routes/patient.py, app/routes/assessments.py, app/routes/schedule.py, app/routes/visits.py, app/routes/coverage.py.
28	  - Data models/persistence: app/models/*.py.
29	  - Observability/audit: app/routes/observability.py, app/routes/audit.py, app/utils/audit.py.
30	  - Static test evidence: tests/*.py.
31	
32	## 4. Section-by-section Review
33	
34	### 4.1 Hard Gates
35	
36	#### 4.1.1 Documentation and static verifiability
37	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
38	- Rationale:
39	  - README provides startup, production env requirements, and test commands with clear structure.
40	  - Entry points and configuration are statically consistent (run.py + app factory + config validation).
41	  - But documented API contract in docs/api-spec.md materially diverges from implemented routes and methods, reducing static verifiability fidelity for reviewers.
42	- Evidence:
43	  - repo/README.md:23
44	  - repo/README.md:80
45	  - repo/run.py:10
46	  - repo/app/__init__.py:88
47	  - docs/api-spec.md:142
48	  - repo/app/routes/coverage.py:64
49	  - docs/api-spec.md:116
50	  - repo/app/routes/visits.py:14
51	  - docs/api-spec.md:100
52	  - repo/app/routes/schedule.py:482
53	- Manual verification note: Not required for this specific conclusion (static mismatch is direct).
54	
55	#### 4.1.2 Material deviation from prompt
56	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
57	- Rationale:
58	  - Core business domains are implemented (assessments, scheduling, visits, coverage, reminders, observability).
59	  - However, some explicit contract/semantics are weakened or changed: login anti-replay not enforced although defined as sensitive; coverage-zone constraints incomplete; API endpoint semantics/path/method drift in docs vs code.
60	- Evidence:
61	  - docs/api-spec.md:23
62	  - repo/app/routes/auth.py:157
63	  - docs/api-spec.md:149
64	  - repo/app/routes/coverage.py:77
65	  - docs/api-spec.md:146
66	  - repo/app/routes/coverage.py:262
67	
68	### 4.2 Delivery Completeness
69	
70	#### 4.2.1 Core explicit requirements coverage
71	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
72	- Rationale:
73	  - Implemented: local auth with username/password, demographics CRUD with masking/encryption, assessment scoring/explanations, 10-minute holds, visit transitions, reminders, audit/observability pages, HTTPS self-signed support.
74	  - Missing/partial vs explicit prompt/API constraints: login anti-replay enforcement; coverage-zone ZIP uniqueness/non-negative validations and full zone update/deactivate paths; API contract parity.
75	- Evidence:
76	  - repo/app/routes/patient.py:122
77	  - repo/app/utils/scoring.py:52
78	  - repo/app/routes/schedule.py:81
79	  - repo/app/models/scheduling.py:108
80	  - repo/app/utils/antireplay.py:14
81	  - repo/app/routes/auth.py:157
82	  - repo/app/routes/coverage.py:86
83	  - repo/app/routes/coverage.py:119
84	
85	#### 4.2.2 End-to-end 0-to-1 deliverable completeness
86	- Conclusion: Pass
87	- Rationale:
88	  - Full project structure exists (app, models, routes, templates, tests, Docker files, docs).
89	  - No evidence of single-file demo-only delivery.
90	  - Real persistence models and extensive test files are present.
91	- Evidence:
92	  - repo/README.md:178
93	  - repo/app/__init__.py:88
94	  - repo/tests/test_assessments.py:130
95	  - repo/tests/test_scheduling.py:61
96	
97	### 4.3 Engineering and Architecture Quality
98	
99	#### 4.3.1 Module decomposition and structure
100	- Conclusion: Pass
101	- Rationale:
102	  - Reasonable Flask modular decomposition: blueprints, models, utilities, templates.
103	  - Security and cross-cutting concerns are centralized in middleware/utilities.
104	- Evidence:
105	  - repo/app/__init__.py:88
106	  - repo/app/utils/middleware.py:11
107	  - repo/app/utils/auth.py:10
108	
109	#### 4.3.2 Maintainability/extensibility
110	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
111	- Rationale:
112	  - Positive: model decomposition, template-driven UI, utility helpers.
113	  - Concerns: docs-to-code API drift increases maintenance risk; observability slow-query implementation is request-duration based and may not map to true DB slow queries; some required business constraints rely on app logic without strong schema-level guards.
114	- Evidence:
115	  - docs/api-spec.md:138
116	  - repo/app/routes/coverage.py:64
117	  - repo/app/utils/middleware.py:60
118	  - repo/app/models/coverage.py:11
119	
120	### 4.4 Engineering Details and Professionalism
121	
122	#### 4.4.1 Error handling, logging, validation, API design
123	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
124	- Rationale:
125	  - Positive: structured JSON logging, correlation IDs, security headers, role-based checks, anti-replay utility, idempotency hashing.
126	  - Gaps: sensitive login endpoint misses anti-replay decorator; some input constraints not enforced (coverage price/distance/ZIP uniqueness rules); API docs and actual endpoints diverge.
127	- Evidence:
128	  - repo/app/utils/logging.py:7
129	  - repo/app/utils/middleware.py:43
130	  - repo/app/utils/antireplay.py:14
131	  - repo/app/routes/auth.py:157
132	  - repo/app/routes/coverage.py:86
133	  - docs/api-spec.md:138
134	
135	#### 4.4.2 Product-like organization vs demo
136	- Conclusion: Pass
137	- Rationale:
138	  - Repository resembles a productized service with layered modules, templates, security utilities, and broad automated tests.
139	- Evidence:
140	  - repo/README.md:178
141	  - repo/app/routes
142	  - repo/tests/test_acceptance_audit.py:1
143	
144	### 4.5 Prompt Understanding and Requirement Fit
145	
146	#### 4.5.1 Business goal and constraints fit
147	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
148	- Rationale:
149	  - Broad alignment is strong for clinic workflow features.
150	  - Material misses remain in explicit security and coverage-zone constraints, and in published API contract consistency.
151	- Evidence:
152	  - repo/app/routes/assessments.py:112
153	  - repo/app/routes/schedule.py:81
154	  - repo/app/routes/visits.py:44
155	  - repo/app/routes/coverage.py:77
156	  - repo/app/routes/auth.py:157
157	  - docs/api-spec.md:23
158	
159	### 4.6 Aesthetics (frontend)
160	
161	#### 4.6.1 Visual/interaction design quality
162	- Conclusion: Cannot Confirm Statistically
163	- Rationale:
164	  - Static template/CSS files exist, but final visual quality, interaction affordances, and consistency require live browser rendering and interaction.
165	- Evidence:
166	  - repo/app/templates/base.html
167	  - repo/app/static/css/style.css
168	- Manual verification note:
169	  - Manual UI walkthrough required across desktop/mobile and major workflows.
170	
171	## 5. Issues / Suggestions (Severity-Rated)
172	
173	### Blocker / High
174	
175	1) Severity: High
176	- Title: Login endpoint does not enforce anti-replay despite sensitive-action requirement
177	- Conclusion: Fail
178	- Evidence:
179	  - docs/api-spec.md:23
180	  - docs/api-spec.md:215
181	  - repo/app/routes/auth.py:157
182	  - repo/app/routes/auth.py:230
183	- Impact:
184	  - Login is a sensitive action but lacks nonce/timestamp/signature protection while other sensitive mutations enforce it. This weakens consistency of replay defenses and violates stated security contract.
185	- Minimum actionable fix:
186	  - Apply anti-replay enforcement to POST /auth/login (decorator or equivalent verification path) and add tests for missing nonce, invalid signature, replay, and valid signed login.
187	
188	2) Severity: High
189	- Title: Coverage zone business constraints incomplete (ZIP uniqueness, non-negative economics, full zone lifecycle)
190	- Conclusion: Fail
191	- Evidence:
192	  - docs/api-spec.md:149
193	  - docs/api-spec.md:144
194	  - docs/api-spec.md:145
195	  - repo/app/routes/coverage.py:77
196	  - repo/app/routes/coverage.py:86
197	  - repo/app/routes/coverage.py:89
198	  - repo/app/routes/coverage.py:119
199	  - repo/app/models/coverage.py:11
200	- Impact:
201	  - Prompt-critical zone correctness can be violated (duplicate ZIP across active zones, negative fees/minimums), and expected update/deactivate lifecycle for zones is incomplete.
202	- Minimum actionable fix:
203	  - Add server-side validation for non-negative min_order_amount/delivery_fee and coherent distance bands.
204	  - Enforce active-zone ZIP exclusivity (application validation plus robust DB strategy).
205	  - Implement zone update/deactivate endpoints matching documented behavior or update docs to exact implemented contract.
206	
207	3) Severity: High
208	- Title: Scheduling self-service hold/confirm routes are not role-scoped to patient/front-desk semantics
209	- Conclusion: Partial Fail
210	- Evidence:
211	  - docs/api-spec.md:96
212	  - repo/app/routes/schedule.py:81
213	  - repo/app/routes/schedule.py:82
214	  - repo/app/routes/schedule.py:109
215	- Impact:
216	  - Any authenticated role can create a reservation as themselves through self-service hold flow, diverging from intended patient/front-desk operational boundary and risking data-shape inconsistencies.
217	- Minimum actionable fix:
218	  - Restrict self-service hold/confirm/cancel to patient role, and require staff to use on-behalf routes only.
219	
220	4) Severity: High
221	- Title: Published API specification materially diverges from implemented routes/methods
222	- Conclusion: Fail
223	- Evidence:
224	  - docs/api-spec.md:142 vs repo/app/routes/coverage.py:64
225	  - docs/api-spec.md:146 vs repo/app/routes/coverage.py:262
226	  - docs/api-spec.md:116 vs repo/app/routes/visits.py:14
227	  - docs/api-spec.md:98 vs repo/app/routes/schedule.py:194
228	  - docs/api-spec.md:100 vs repo/app/routes/schedule.py:482
229	  - docs/api-spec.md:101 vs repo/app/routes/schedule.py:440
230	  - docs/api-spec.md:201 and docs/api-spec.md:203 vs repo/app/routes/observability.py:47
231	- Impact:
232	  - Hard-gate documentation verifiability is weakened; consumers/reviewers cannot trust API docs for static validation.
233	- Minimum actionable fix:
234	  - Reconcile docs and implementation one-to-one (path, method, auth, request/response semantics), then add contract tests for documented endpoints.
235	
236	### Medium
237	
238	5) Severity: Medium
239	- Title: Observability slow-query feature records slow requests, not explicit database query statements
240	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
241	- Evidence:
242	  - repo/app/utils/middleware.py:60
243	  - repo/app/models/audit.py:32
244	- Impact:
245	  - Operational diagnosis may miss true SQL hotspots and may conflate network/template latency with DB slow queries.
246	- Minimum actionable fix:
247	  - Instrument SQLAlchemy query timing hooks and persist query metadata safely (with parameter redaction).
248	
249	6) Severity: Medium
250	- Title: Delivery-check endpoint is publicly accessible although API contract states patient/front-desk auth
251	- Conclusion: Partial Fail
252	- Evidence:
253	  - docs/api-spec.md:146
254	  - repo/app/routes/coverage.py:262
255	- Impact:
256	  - Information exposure and contract inconsistency for coverage eligibility behavior.
257	- Minimum actionable fix:
258	  - Add role/auth guard per intended contract or explicitly document this endpoint as intentionally public.
259	
260	7) Severity: Medium
261	- Title: Medication adherence implementation is a single-select field, weaker than prompt wording of 4-question scale
262	- Conclusion: Partial Fail
263	- Evidence:
264	  - docs/api-spec.md:88
265	  - repo/app/utils/scoring.py:171
266	  - repo/app/utils/scoring.py:179
267	- Impact:
268	  - Reduced assessment granularity relative to stated requirement semantics.
269	- Minimum actionable fix:
270	  - Expand adherence section to 4 questions (or formally align requirement text/docs with implemented single-question approach).
271	
272	### Low
273	
274	8) Severity: Low
275	- Title: API status semantics for login rate limiting are UI-message oriented rather than explicit 429 behavior in tests
276	- Conclusion: Cannot Confirm Statistically
277	- Evidence:
278	  - docs/api-spec.md:29
279	  - repo/app/routes/auth.py:172
280	  - repo/tests/test_auth.py:155
281	- Impact:
282	  - Contract ambiguity for API clients expecting strict 429 semantics.
283	- Minimum actionable fix:
284	  - Return explicit 429 for API/HTMX lockout paths and add status-code assertions.
285	
286	## 6. Security Review Summary
287	
288	- Authentication entry points
289	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
290	  - Evidence: repo/app/routes/auth.py:106, repo/app/routes/auth.py:157, repo/app/routes/auth.py:168
291	  - Reasoning: Registration/login/rate-limit exist; login lacks anti-replay enforcement required for sensitive actions.
292	
293	- Route-level authorization
294	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
295	  - Evidence: repo/app/utils/auth.py:10, repo/app/routes/admin.py:14, repo/app/routes/visits.py:45
296	  - Reasoning: Widespread role_required usage, but some business-specific role boundaries are looser than prompt semantics.
297	
298	- Object-level authorization
299	  - Conclusion: Pass
300	  - Evidence: repo/app/routes/visits.py:90, repo/app/routes/schedule.py:125, repo/app/routes/patient.py:189, repo/tests/test_user_isolation.py:109
301	  - Reasoning: Key object-owner checks exist for timelines, reservations, and patient-sensitive data.
302	
303	- Function-level authorization
304	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
305	  - Evidence: repo/app/routes/schedule.py:81, repo/app/routes/schedule.py:234, repo/app/routes/coverage.py:67
306	  - Reasoning: Most function gates are present; self-service scheduling functions are not tightly role-scoped.
307	
308	- Tenant / user isolation
309	  - Conclusion: Pass
310	  - Evidence: repo/app/routes/assessments.py:184, repo/app/routes/notes.py:52, repo/tests/test_user_isolation.py:84
311	  - Reasoning: Owner filtering and dedicated isolation tests are present for major patient data flows.
312	
313	- Admin / internal / debug endpoint protection
314	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
315	  - Evidence: repo/app/routes/health.py:22, repo/app/routes/observability.py:14, repo/app/routes/audit.py:8
316	  - Reasoning: Admin guard is present; however, documented admin operations sub-endpoints are not fully implemented as specified.
317	
318	## 7. Tests and Logging Review
319	
320	- Unit tests
321	  - Conclusion: Pass
322	  - Evidence: repo/tests/test_assessments.py:52, repo/tests/test_visits.py:51, repo/tests/test_notes.py:27
323	
324	- API / integration tests
325	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
326	  - Evidence: repo/tests/test_security.py:25, repo/tests/test_acceptance_audit.py:128, repo/tests/test_scheduling.py:61
327	  - Reasoning: Strong coverage across core flows and many failure paths; notable gaps remain around login anti-replay and some coverage-zone constraints.
328	
329	- Logging categories / observability
330	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
331	  - Evidence: repo/app/utils/logging.py:7, repo/app/utils/middleware.py:60, repo/app/routes/observability.py:14
332	
333	- Sensitive-data leakage risk in logs / responses
334	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
335	  - Evidence: repo/app/models/clinical_note.py:13, repo/app/models/clinical_note.py:37, repo/app/utils/audit.py:28
336	  - Reasoning: Clinical content encrypted at rest and not logged directly by design. Manual verification required for complete response-surface leak assurance in all templates and exceptional paths.
337	
338	## 8. Test Coverage Assessment (Static Audit)
339	
340	### 8.1 Test Overview
341	- Unit and integration tests exist under tests/.
342	- E2E tests also exist under tests/e2e/ (not executed).
343	- Framework: pytest.
344	- Test entry points documented in README and run_tests.sh.
345	- Evidence:
346	  - repo/tests/conftest.py:1
347	  - repo/tests/test_assessments.py:1
348	  - repo/tests/test_scheduling.py:1
349	  - repo/tests/e2e/test_auth.py:1
350	  - repo/README.md:80
351	  - repo/README.md:86
352	  - repo/run_tests.sh:1
353	
354	### 8.2 Coverage Mapping Table
355	
356	| Requirement / Risk Point | Mapped Test Case(s) | Key Assertion / Fixture / Mock | Coverage Assessment | Gap | Minimum Test Addition |
357	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
358	| Auth login + rate limit | tests/test_auth.py:86, tests/test_auth.py:155 | Lockout message after repeated failures | Basically covered | No anti-replay assertions on login | Add tests for missing nonce, invalid signature, replay, valid signed login on POST /auth/login |
359	| CSRF + anti-replay on sensitive mutations | tests/test_acceptance_audit.py:128, tests/test_security.py:192 | Missing nonce returns 400; replay returns 409 | Sufficient (for covered endpoints) | Login endpoint not covered by anti-replay tests | Extend anti-replay suite to include login endpoint |
360	| RBAC route protections | tests/test_rbac.py:25, tests/test_rbac.py:42 | Non-admin gets 403 on admin users page | Sufficient | Role-boundary semantics for self-service scheduling not asserted | Add role-matrix tests for /schedule/hold and /schedule/confirm |
361	| Object-level authorization and user isolation | tests/test_user_isolation.py:109, tests/test_acceptance_audit.py:84 | User B denied for User A objects/timeline | Sufficient | None material observed | Keep regression tests for new endpoints |
362	| Assessment scoring and explainability | tests/test_assessments.py:52, tests/test_assessments.py:60 | Risk-level and score assertions | Sufficient | Medication adherence modeled as single select not 4-question scale | Add tests for 4-question adherence once implemented |
363	| Idempotent transitions and submission | tests/test_visits.py:150, tests/test_assessments.py:155 | Duplicate token does not double-process | Sufficient | None material observed | Add negative tests for malformed token shape |
364	| Scheduling hold expiry and booking lifecycle | tests/test_scheduling.py:169, tests/test_scheduling.py:287, tests/test_scheduling.py:420 | Expire stale holds + visit creation on confirm | Sufficient | Contract mismatch on route methods/paths not validated | Add contract-level route-method assertions against docs |
365	| Coverage zones business constraints | tests/test_coverage.py:53, tests/test_coverage.py:129 | Zone creation + window overlap checks | Insufficient | No tests for ZIP uniqueness across active zones, non-negative min/fee enforcement, full zone update/deactivate semantics | Add tests for duplicate ZIP cross-zone rejection, negative fee/min rejection, zone soft-deactivate flow |
366	| Data export + deletion anonymization | tests/test_security.py:107, tests/test_delete_account.py:129 | Export payload checks, anonymization retention/scrub assertions | Basically covered | Manual legal retention semantics still require domain review | Add explicit audit-log retention schema assertions per policy |
367	| Observability/admin operations | tests/test_observability.py:23, tests/test_audit_security.py:180 | Admin-gated observability and slow-request persistence | Basically covered | Missing tests for documented operations sub-endpoints that are absent in code | Add route-contract tests for /admin/operations/alerts, /slow-queries, /sessions |
368	
369	### 8.3 Security Coverage Audit
370	- authentication
371	  - Conclusion: Basically covered
372	  - Evidence: tests/test_auth.py:86, tests/test_auth.py:155
373	  - Gap: anti-replay for login is not covered and not implemented.
374	- route authorization
375	  - Conclusion: Sufficient
376	  - Evidence: tests/test_rbac.py:25, tests/test_acceptance_audit.py:69
377	- object-level authorization
378	  - Conclusion: Sufficient
379	  - Evidence: tests/test_user_isolation.py:109, tests/test_security.py:242
380	- tenant / data isolation
381	  - Conclusion: Sufficient
382	  - Evidence: tests/test_user_isolation.py:84, tests/test_notes.py:143
383	- admin / internal protection
384	  - Conclusion: Basically covered
385	  - Evidence: tests/test_observability.py:23, tests/test_observability.py:74
386	  - Gap: incomplete implementation of documented admin operations sub-endpoints can leave defects undetected.
387	
388	### 8.4 Final Coverage Judgment
389	- Final coverage judgment: Partial Pass
390	- Boundary explanation:
391	  - Covered well: core auth flows, RBAC, anti-replay for several sensitive endpoints, assessment scoring/idempotency, scheduling lifecycle, user isolation, deletion anonymization.
392	  - Not covered enough: login anti-replay behavior, coverage-zone constraint enforcement (ZIP uniqueness and non-negative economics), and route-contract parity for documented admin/coverage/dashboard API surfaces.
393	  - Therefore tests could still pass while severe defects remain in these uncovered/misaligned areas.
394	
395	## 9. Final Notes
396	- This audit is static-only and evidence-based; no runtime claims were made.
397	- The strongest blockers are security/control and contract-alignment defects, not overall project structure.
398	- The repository is close to acceptable, but high-severity requirement/security mismatches should be resolved before delivery acceptance.

content.text:
1	{
2	    "prompt": "A MeridianCare Clinic Operations platform capable of running entirely on an internal workstation or clinic LAN provides end-to-end patient intake, triage, and appointment execution without relying on any external services. Patients and front-desk staff use an English-language HTMX-driven web interface for fast, form-based workflows: creating an account with a username and password (no SMS/email codes), maintaining basic demographics, and completing pre-visit health assessments that include common scales (e.g., PHQ-9, GAD-7) plus clinic-defined indicators such as blood pressure category, fall-risk flags, and medication adherence. The assessment screen shows immediate, explainable risk stratification (e.g., Low/Moderate/High) with the specific rules and contributing answers displayed, then generates a traceable assessment record tied to the visit. Scheduling supports clinician and room/resource calendars with slot capacity (default 1 patient per 15-minute slot per clinician), conflict detection, holiday closures, bulk template scheduling, and a reservation hold that expires after 10 minutes if the patient abandons checkout. Visits move through a guided state machine visible on a shared dashboard—Booked, Pending Payment, Checked In, Seen, Canceled, or No-Show—with clear UI feedback for locked states, duplicate-click prevention, and timestamped milestones for operational reporting. For clinics that offer in-house supply drop-off or mobile services, administrators configure service coverage zones by neighborhood/ZIP grouping and distance bands from the clinic, setting per-zone minimum order thresholds (e.g., $25.00), flat delivery fees (e.g., $5.00), and allowable delivery windows (e.g., 9:00 AM–12:00 PM, 1:00 PM–5:00 PM) to avoid overpromising outside coverage. The backend uses Flask to implement REST-style endpoints consumed by HTMX partial updates, enforcing idempotent transitions with request tokens, server-side validation, and auditable change logs that record who changed what, when, and why. SQLite provides offline persistence for accounts, roles (Administrator, Clinician, Front Desk, Patient), visit orders and state transitions, assessment definitions and versions, assessment results with explanation snapshots, scheduling resources and holds, zone configurations, and operational telemetry. Automated periodic reassessments and reminders run via a local scheduler within the app process, defaulting to every 90 days for chronic-care templates and 24 hours before a booked visit, with reminders shown in-app at login rather than sent externally. Security and privacy controls assume a closed network but still require HTTPS (self-signed certificates allowed), CSRF protection, strict input validation to prevent SQL injection and XSS, signed request timestamps with a 5-minute anti-replay window for sensitive actions, and rate limiting (e.g., 10 login attempts per 10 minutes per account plus IP). Sensitive fields are classified and protected with masking in the UI (e.g., showing only last 4 of an ID), encryption at rest for stored tokens and clinical notes, and encryption in transit. Users can export their personal data locally as a file and request deletion; deletions anonymize clinical records while preserving legally required audit trails. Observability is fully local: structured logs, slow-query reporting, request correlation IDs for traceability, lightweight health checks, and anomaly alerts (e.g., repeated failed logins, unusual new-device sessions) displayed on an admin operations screen.",

1	import hmac
2	import hashlib
3	from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
4	from flask import request, current_app, jsonify
5	from functools import wraps
6	from app.extensions import db
7	from app.models.audit import SignedRequest
8	
9	
10	def _hash_nonce(nonce: str) -> str:
11	    """One-way hash a nonce for safe at-rest storage."""
12	    return hashlib.sha256(nonce.encode()).hexdigest()
13	
14	REPLAY_WINDOW = timedelta(minutes=5)
15	
16	
17	def _compute_signature(secret: str, method: str, path: str, nonce: str, timestamp: str) -> str:
18	    """HMAC-SHA256 over 'METHOD|path|nonce|timestamp' using the server signing secret."""
19	    payload = f"{method.upper()}|{path}|{nonce}|{timestamp}"
20	    return hmac.new(secret.encode(), payload.encode(), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
21	
22	
23	def antireplay(f):
24	    @wraps(f)
25	    def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
26	        # Only enforce anti-replay on state-mutating methods.
27	        if request.method not in ("POST", "PUT", "PATCH", "DELETE"):
28	            return f(*args, **kwargs)
29	
30	        # Lazily purge expired nonces so the table doesn't grow unbounded.
31	        try:
32	            SignedRequest.query.filter(
33	                SignedRequest.expires_at < datetime.now(timezone.utc)
34	            ).delete()
35	            db.session.commit()
36	        except Exception:
37	            db.session.rollback()
38	
39	        nonce = request.headers.get("X-Nonce") or request.form.get("_nonce")
40	        ts_header = request.headers.get("X-Timestamp") or request.form.get("_timestamp")
41	        signature = request.headers.get("X-Signature") or request.form.get("_signature")
42	
43	        if not nonce or not ts_header:
44	            return jsonify({"error": "Missing nonce or timestamp"}), 400
45	
46	        if not signature:
47	            return jsonify({"error": "Missing request signature"}), 400
48	
49	        try:
50	            ts = datetime.fromisoformat(ts_header)
51	            if ts.tzinfo is None:
52	                ts = ts.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
53	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
54	            return jsonify({"error": "Request expired, please try again"}), 400
55	
56	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
57	        if abs(now - ts) > REPLAY_WINDOW:
58	            return jsonify({"error": "Request expired, please try again"}), 400
59	
60	        # Signature verification — must happen before the nonce is stored so
61	        # an attacker cannot use a 409 response to probe whether a nonce exists.
62	        secret = current_app.config.get("REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET", "")
63	        expected = _compute_signature(secret, request.method, request.path, nonce, ts_header)
64	        if not hmac.compare_digest(signature, expected):
65	            return jsonify({"error": "Invalid request signature"}), 400
66	
67	        # Replay check — nonce must not have been seen before.
68	        # Store and compare the SHA-256 hash so raw nonces never reach the DB.
69	        nonce_hash = _hash_nonce(nonce)
70	        existing = SignedRequest.query.filter_by(nonce=nonce_hash).first()
71	        if existing:
72	            return jsonify({"error": "Request already processed"}), 409
73	
74	        sr = SignedRequest(nonce=nonce_hash, timestamp=ts, expires_at=ts + REPLAY_WINDOW)
75	        db.session.add(sr)
76	        db.session.commit()
77	        return f(*args, **kwargs)
78	    return decorated
79

16	def _parse_time(value: str):
17	    """Parse an HH:MM string into a time object, returning None on failure."""
18	    try:
19	        parts = value.strip().split(":")
20	        return dt_time(int(parts[0]), int(parts[1]))
21	    except (ValueError, IndexError, AttributeError):
22	        return None

25	def _validate_window_fields(day, start_str, end_str):
26	    """Validate day/time fields shared by create and update.
27	
28	    Returns (start_time, end_time, errors) where errors is a list of
29	    human-readable strings. start_time/end_time are None when parsing failed.
30	    """
31	    errors = []
32	    if day not in VALID_DAYS:
33	        errors.append(
34	            f"Invalid day '{day}'. Must be one of: {', '.join(sorted(VALID_DAYS))}."
35	        )
36	
37	    start_time = _parse_time(start_str)
38	    end_time = _parse_time(end_str)
39	
40	    if start_time is None:
41	        errors.append("Invalid start time format — use HH:MM.")
42	    if end_time is None:
43	        errors.append("Invalid end time format — use HH:MM.")
44	    if start_time and end_time and start_time >= end_time:
45	        errors.append("Start time must be before end time.")
46	
47	    return start_time, end_time, errors

50	def _has_overlap(zone_id, day, start_time, end_time, exclude_id=None):
51	    """Return True if any window for zone_id/day overlaps the given time range.
52	
53	    Overlap rule: existing.start < new_end AND new_start < existing.end.
54	    Pass exclude_id to skip the window being updated.
55	    """
56	    query = ZoneDeliveryWindow.query.filter_by(zone_id=zone_id, day_of_week=day)
57	    if exclude_id is not None:
58	        query = query.filter(ZoneDeliveryWindow.id != exclude_id)
59	    for w in query.all():
60	        if w.start_time < end_time and start_time < w.end_time:
61	            return True
62	    return False

262	@coverage_bp.route("/check")
263	def check_coverage():
264	    """Check coverage for a given ZIP code, optional neighborhood, and optional distance.
265	
266	    Query parameters:
267	      zip          – ZIP code string (required unless neighborhood is supplied)
268	      neighborhood – neighborhood name string (optional, alternative/additional to zip)
269	      distance     – non-negative float in miles from clinic (optional)
270	
271	    Distance band logic:
272	      A zone whose distance_band_max > 0 will only be returned when the caller
273	      provides a `distance` value that falls within [distance_band_min, distance_band_max].
274	      Zones with distance_band_max == 0 (or null) have no distance constraint.
275	
276	    Returns HTTP 400 for missing location identifiers or a non-numeric distance value.
277	    """
278	    zip_code = request.args.get("zip", "").strip()
279	    neighborhood = request.args.get("neighborhood", "").strip()
280	    distance_str = request.args.get("distance", "").strip()
281	
282	    # Require at least one location identifier.
283	    if not zip_code and not neighborhood:
284	        return jsonify({"covered": False, "error": "zip or neighborhood is required"}), 400
285	
286	    # Validate and parse distance if provided.
287	    distance = None
288	    if distance_str:
289	        try:
290	            distance = float(distance_str)
291	        except ValueError:
292	            return jsonify({"covered": False, "error": "distance must be a number"}), 400
293	        if distance < 0:
294	            return jsonify({"covered": False, "error": "distance must be non-negative"}), 400
295	
296	    zones = CoverageZone.query.filter_by(is_active=True).all()
297	    matching = []
298	    for zone in zones:
299	        zips = zone.zip_codes_json or []
300	        neighborhoods = zone.neighborhoods_json or []
301	
302	        # Location check: ZIP or neighborhood must match at least one configured value.
303	        zip_match = bool(zip_code) and zip_code in zips
304	        nbhd_match = bool(neighborhood) and neighborhood in neighborhoods
305	
306	        if not zip_match and not nbhd_match:
307	            continue
308	
309	        # Distance band check: only applied when the zone has a configured max band.
310	        band_max = zone.distance_band_max or 0
311	        if band_max > 0:
312	            if distance is None:
313	                # Zone requires a distance but caller did not provide one — skip.
314	                continue
315	            band_min = zone.distance_band_min or 0
316	            if not (band_min <= distance <= band_max):
317	                continue
318	
319	        windows = []
320	        for w in zone.delivery_windows.all():
321	            windows.append({
322	                "day_of_week": w.day_of_week,
323	                "start_time": w.start_time.strftime("%H:%M") if w.start_time else None,
324	                "end_time": w.end_time.strftime("%H:%M") if w.end_time else None,
325	            })
326	
327	        matching.append({
328	            "id": zone.id,
329	            "name": zone.name,
330	            "delivery_fee": zone.delivery_fee,
331	            "min_order_amount": zone.min_order_amount,
332	            "distance_band_min": zone.distance_band_min,
333	            "distance_band_max": zone.distance_band_max,
334	            "delivery_windows": windows,
335	        })
336	
337	    if matching:
338	        return jsonify({"covered": True, "zones": matching})
339	    return jsonify({"covered": False, "zones": []})
340

20	    """Return True if a slot already occupies room_id during [start_time, end_time) on slot_date."""
21	    if room_id is None:

23	    query = Slot.query.filter(

154	    if not reservation or reservation.patient_id != current_user.id:
155	        flash("Reservation not found.", "danger")
156	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.available"))
157	
158	    if reservation.is_expired():
159	        reservation.status = "expired"
160	        db.session.commit()
161	        flash("Your reservation hold has expired.", "warning")
162	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.available"))
163	
164	    if reservation.status == "confirmed":
165	        flash("This reservation is already confirmed.", "info")
166	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.my_appointments"))
167	
168	    reservation.status = "confirmed"
169	    reservation.confirmed_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
170	    reservation.slot.booked_count += 1
171	
172	    # Idempotently create a Visit record for this booking.
173	    # Using slot_id + patient_id as the uniqueness key prevents duplicates
174	    # even if confirm is somehow called twice on separate requests.
175	    existing_visit = Visit.query.filter_by(
176	        patient_id=reservation.patient_id,
177	        slot_id=reservation.slot_id,
178	    ).first()
179	    if not existing_visit:
180	        visit = Visit(
181	            patient_id=reservation.patient_id,
182	            clinician_id=reservation.slot.clinician_id,
183	            slot_id=reservation.slot_id,
184	            status="booked",
185	        )
186	        db.session.add(visit)
187	
188	    db.session.commit()
189	
190	    flash("Appointment confirmed!", "success")
191	    return redirect(url_for("schedule.my_appointments"))

406

408	        try:
409	            week_start = datetime.strptime(week_str, "%Y-%m-%d").date()
410	        except ValueError:
411	            week_start = date.today() - timedelta(days=date.today().weekday())
412	    else:
413	        week_start = date.today() - timedelta(days=date.today().weekday())
414

418	    if clinician_id:

425

427	    next_week = (week_start + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat()
428

482	@schedule_bp.route("/admin/bulk-generate", methods=["GET", "POST"])
483	@role_required("administrator")
484	@antireplay
485	def bulk_generate():
486	    clinicians = Clinician.query.all()
487	
488	    rooms = Room.query.filter_by(is_active=True).order_by(Room.name).all()
489	
490	    if request.method == "POST":
491	        clinician_id = request.form.get("clinician_id", type=int)
492	        date_from = request.form.get("date_from", "").strip()
493	        date_to = request.form.get("date_to", "").strip()
494	        room_id = request.form.get("room_id", type=int) or None
495	
496	        if not clinician_id or not date_from or not date_to:
497	            flash("All fields are required.", "danger")
498	            return render_template("schedule/bulk_generate.html", clinicians=clinicians, rooms=rooms)
499	
500	        try:
501	            d_from = datetime.strptime(date_from, "%Y-%m-%d").date()
502	            d_to = datetime.strptime(date_to, "%Y-%m-%d").date()
503	        except ValueError:
504	            flash("Invalid dates.", "danger")
505	            return render_template("schedule/bulk_generate.html", clinicians=clinicians, rooms=rooms)
506	
507	        templates = ScheduleTemplate.query.filter_by(clinician_id=clinician_id).all()
508	        if not templates:
509	            flash("No schedule template found for this clinician.", "warning")
510	            return render_template("schedule/bulk_generate.html", clinicians=clinicians, rooms=rooms)
511	
512	        holiday_dates = {h.date for h in Holiday.query.filter(Holiday.date >= d_from, Holiday.date <= d_to).all()}
513	
514	        created = 0
515	        skipped_conflicts = 0
516	        current = d_from
517	        while current <= d_to:
518	            if current in holiday_dates:
519	                current += timedelta(days=1)
520	                continue
521	
522	            dow = current.weekday()
523	            for tmpl in templates:
524	                if tmpl.day_of_week != dow:
525	                    continue
526	
527	                # Generate slots
528	                t = datetime.combine(current, tmpl.start_time)
529	                end = datetime.combine(current, tmpl.end_time)
530	                while t < end:
531	                    slot_end_time = (t + timedelta(minutes=tmpl.slot_duration)).time()
532	                    existing = Slot.query.filter_by(
533	                        clinician_id=clinician_id, date=current, start_time=t.time()
534	                    ).first()
535	                    if not existing:
536	                        if _has_room_conflict(room_id, current, t.time(), slot_end_time):
537	                            skipped_conflicts += 1
538	                        else:
539	                            slot = Slot(
540	                                clinician_id=clinician_id,
541	                                room_id=room_id,
542	                                date=current,
543	                                start_time=t.time(),
544	                                end_time=slot_end_time,
545	                                capacity=tmpl.capacity,
546	                            )
547	                            db.session.add(slot)
548	                            created += 1
549	                    t += timedelta(minutes=tmpl.slot_duration)
550	
551	            current += timedelta(days=1)
552	
553	        db.session.commit()
554	        msg = f"Generated {created} slots."
555	        if skipped_conflicts:
556	            msg += f" Skipped {skipped_conflicts} slot(s) due to room conflicts."
557	        flash(msg, "success")
558	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.bulk_generate"))
559	
560	    return render_template("schedule/bulk_generate.html", clinicians=clinicians, rooms=rooms)
561

1	import uuid
2	import time
3	from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
4	from flask import g, request, session, redirect, url_for, flash
5	from flask_login import current_user, logout_user
6	import logging
7	
8	SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES = 30
9	
10	
11	def register_middleware(app):
12	    @app.before_request
13	    def set_correlation_id():
14	        g.correlation_id = request.headers.get("X-Correlation-ID", str(uuid.uuid4()))
15	
16	    @app.before_request
17	    def record_request_start():
18	        g.request_start_time = time.time()
19	
20	    @app.before_request
21	    def check_session_timeout():
22	        exempt_paths = ("/auth/login", "/auth/register", "/auth/logout", "/health", "/static")
23	        if any(request.path.startswith(p) for p in exempt_paths):
24	            return
25	        if current_user and hasattr(current_user, "is_authenticated") and current_user.is_authenticated:
26	            last_active = session.get("last_active")
27	            now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
28	            if last_active:
29	                try:
30	                    last_dt = datetime.fromisoformat(last_active)
31	                    if last_dt.tzinfo is None:
32	                        last_dt = last_dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
33	                    if now - last_dt > timedelta(minutes=SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES):
34	                        logout_user()
35	                        flash("Session expired due to inactivity.", "warning")
36	                        return redirect(url_for("auth.login"))
37	                except (ValueError, TypeError):
38	                    pass
39	            session["last_active"] = now.isoformat()
40	
41	    @app.after_request
42	    def add_security_headers(response):
43	        response.headers["X-Content-Type-Options"] = "nosniff"
44	        response.headers["X-Frame-Options"] = "DENY"
45	        response.headers["X-XSS-Protection"] = "1; mode=block"
46	        response.headers["Strict-Transport-Security"] = "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains"
47	        response.headers["Content-Security-Policy"] = "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'"
48	        return response
49	
50	    @app.after_request
51	    def log_request(response):
52	        correlation_id = getattr(g, "correlation_id", "unknown")
53	        response.headers["X-Correlation-ID"] = correlation_id
54	        logger = logging.getLogger("meridiancare.request")
55	        extra = {"correlation_id": correlation_id}
56	
57	        start_time = getattr(g, "request_start_time", None)
58	        if start_time is not None:
59	            duration_ms = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000
60	            if duration_ms > 500:
61	                logger.warning(
62	                    "%s %s %s duration=%.1fms (SLOW)",
63	                    request.method, request.path, response.status_code, duration_ms,
64	                    extra=extra,
65	                )
66	                # Persist slow-query record for admin operations UI.
67	                try:
68	                    from app.models.audit import SlowQuery
69	                    from app.extensions import db
70	                    sq = SlowQuery(
71	                        endpoint=request.path,
72	                        duration_ms=duration_ms,
73	                        correlation_id=correlation_id,
74	                    )
75	                    db.session.add(sq)
76	                    db.session.commit()
77	                except Exception:
78	                    try:
79	                        from app.extensions import db
80	                        db.session.rollback()
81	                    except Exception:
82	                        pass
83	            else:
84	                logger.info(
85	                    "%s %s %s duration=%.1fms",
86	                    request.method, request.path, response.status_code, duration_ms,
87	                    extra=extra,
88	                )
89	        else:
90	            logger.info(
91	                "%s %s %s", request.method, request.path, response.status_code, extra=extra
92	            )
93	        return response

1	"""Risk stratification scoring engine."""
2	
3	import json
4	
5	
6	def calculate_scores(answers):
7	    """Calculate individual scale scores from answers dict."""
8	    scores = {}
9	
10	    # PHQ-9: sum of 9 questions (0-3 each, total 0-27)
11	    phq9_total = sum(int(answers.get(f"phq9_q{i}", 0)) for i in range(1, 10))
12	    if phq9_total <= 4:
13	        phq9_severity = "Minimal"
14	    elif phq9_total <= 9:
15	        phq9_severity = "Mild"
16	    elif phq9_total <= 14:
17	        phq9_severity = "Moderate"
18	    elif phq9_total <= 19:
19	        phq9_severity = "Moderately Severe"
20	    else:
21	        phq9_severity = "Severe"
22	    scores["phq9"] = {"total": phq9_total, "severity": phq9_severity}
23	
24	    # GAD-7: sum of 7 questions (0-3 each, total 0-21)
25	    gad7_total = sum(int(answers.get(f"gad7_q{i}", 0)) for i in range(1, 8))
26	    if gad7_total <= 4:
27	        gad7_severity = "Minimal"
28	    elif gad7_total <= 9:
29	        gad7_severity = "Mild"
30	    elif gad7_total <= 14:
31	        gad7_severity = "Moderate"
32	    else:
33	        gad7_severity = "Severe"
34	    scores["gad7"] = {"total": gad7_total, "severity": gad7_severity}
35	
36	    # Blood pressure
37	    bp_category = answers.get("bp_category", "Normal")
38	    scores["blood_pressure"] = {"category": bp_category}
39	
40	    # Fall risk: count of Yes answers
41	    fall_risk_flags = sum(
42	        1 for q in ["fall_history", "mobility_aids", "dizziness", "balance_meds"]
43	        if answers.get(q) == "yes"
44	    )
45	    scores["fall_risk"] = {"flags": fall_risk_flags, "total_questions": 4}
46	
47	    # Medication adherence
48	    adherence = answers.get("med_adherence", "never_miss")
49	    scores["medication_adherence"] = {"level": adherence}
50	
51	    return scores
52	
53	
54	def calculate_risk_level(scores):
55	    """Determine overall risk level and generate explanation."""
56	    risk_level = "Low"
57	    explanations = []
58	
59	    phq9 = scores.get("phq9", {})
60	    gad7 = scores.get("gad7", {})
61	    bp = scores.get("blood_pressure", {})
62	    fall = scores.get("fall_risk", {})
63	    med = scores.get("medication_adherence", {})
64	
65	    # High risk rules
66	    if phq9.get("total", 0) >= 15:
67	        risk_level = "High"
68	        explanations.append(f"PHQ-9 score {phq9['total']} ({phq9['severity']}) >= 15 threshold")
69	
70	    if gad7.get("total", 0) >= 15:
71	        risk_level = "High"
72	        explanations.append(f"GAD-7 score {gad7['total']} ({gad7['severity']}) >= 15 threshold")
73	
74	    if bp.get("category") == "Crisis":
75	        risk_level = "High"
76	        explanations.append("Blood pressure category: Crisis")
77	
78	    if fall.get("flags", 0) >= 2:
79	        if risk_level != "High":
80	            risk_level = "High"
81	        explanations.append(f"Fall risk: {fall['flags']} flags (>= 2 threshold)")
82	
83	    # Moderate risk rules (only upgrade if currently Low)
84	    if risk_level == "Low":
85	        if phq9.get("total", 0) >= 10:
86	            risk_level = "Moderate"
87	            explanations.append(f"PHQ-9 score {phq9['total']} ({phq9['severity']}) >= 10 threshold")
88	
89	        if gad7.get("total", 0) >= 10:
90	            risk_level = "Moderate"
91	            explanations.append(f"GAD-7 score {gad7['total']} ({gad7['severity']}) >= 10 threshold")
92	
93	        if bp.get("category") in ("Stage 1", "Stage 2"):
94	            risk_level = "Moderate"
95	            explanations.append(f"Blood pressure category: {bp['category']}")
96	
97	        if fall.get("flags", 0) >= 1:
98	            risk_level = "Moderate"
99	            explanations.append(f"Fall risk: {fall['flags']} flag(s)")
100	
101	        if med.get("level") in ("sometimes_miss", "often_miss"):
102	            risk_level = "Moderate"
103	            explanations.append(f"Medication adherence: {med['level'].replace('_', ' ')}")
104	
105	    if not explanations:
106	        explanations.append("All indicators within normal range")
107	
108	    return risk_level, explanations
109	
110	
111	# Default assessment template data

1	from functools import wraps
2	from flask import abort, request, jsonify
3	from flask_login import current_user, login_required
4	import logging
5	
6	logger = logging.getLogger("meridiancare.auth")
7	
8	
9	def role_required(*roles):
10	    """Decorator that requires the current user to have one of the specified roles."""
11	    def decorator(f):
12	        @wraps(f)
13	        @login_required
14	        def decorated_function(*args, **kwargs):
15	            if current_user.role not in roles:
16	                logger.warning(
17	                    "Access denied: user=%s role=%s required=%s path=%s",
18	                    current_user.username,
19	                    current_user.role,
20	                    roles,
21	                    request.path,
22	                )
23	                if request.headers.get("HX-Request"):
24	                    return jsonify({"error": "Access denied"}), 403
25	                abort(403)
26	            return f(*args, **kwargs)
27	        return decorated_function
28	    return decorator
29

content.text:
1	# API Specification
2	
3	All endpoints are REST-style, consumed by HTMX partial updates. Responses return HTML fragments for HTMX requests (`HX-Request` header present) and JSON for direct API calls. All state-changing endpoints require CSRF tokens and, for sensitive actions, signed request timestamps with anti-replay nonces.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Health & System
8	
9	| Method | Path | Description | Auth | Request | Response |
10	|--------|------|-------------|------|---------|----------|
11	| GET | `/health` | Basic health check | None | — | `{"status": "ok", "timestamp": "ISO8601"}` |
12	| GET | `/health/detailed` | Extended health (DB, disk, scheduler, encryption key) | Admin | — | `{"status": "ok", "database": "ok", "disk_space_mb": 1024, "scheduler": "running", "encryption_key": "loaded"}` |
13	
14	---
15	
16	## Authentication (`/auth`)
17	
18	| Method | Path | Description | Auth | Request | Response |
19	|--------|------|-------------|------|---------|----------|
20	| GET | `/auth/register` | Registration page | None | — | HTML form |
21	| POST | `/auth/register` | Create account | None | `username`, `password`, `confirm_password` | Redirect to login (success) or HTML partial with errors |
22	| GET | `/auth/login` | Login page | None | — | HTML form |
23	| POST | `/auth/login` | Authenticate | None | `username`, `password`, signed timestamp header | Session cookie + redirect to role dashboard |
24	| POST | `/auth/logout` | End session | Authenticated | CSRF token | Redirect to login |
25	| GET | `/auth/check-username` | HTMX username availability | None | `?username=<value>` | HTML partial: "Available" / "Taken" |
26	
27	### Rate Limiting
28	- 10 login attempts per 10 minutes per account AND per IP address
29	- Exceeding returns 429 with lockout remaining time
30	
31	---
32	
33	## User Management (`/admin/users`) — Admin Only
34	
35	| Method | Path | Description | Auth | Request | Response |
36	|--------|------|-------------|------|---------|----------|
37	| GET | `/admin/users` | List all users | Admin | `?page=`, `?search=` | HTML table with pagination |
38	| PUT | `/admin/users/<id>/role` | Change user role | Admin | `role` (Administrator/Clinician/Front Desk/Patient) | HTML partial (updated row) |
39	| PUT | `/admin/users/<id>/status` | Activate/deactivate user | Admin | `is_active` (bool) | HTML partial (updated row) |
40	
41	### Constraints
42	- Cannot demote the last Administrator
43	- Cannot change own role
44	
45	---
46	
47	## Patient Demographics (`/patient`, `/staff/patients`)
48	
49	| Method | Path | Description | Auth | Request | Response |
50	|--------|------|-------------|------|---------|----------|
51	| GET | `/patient/demographics` | View own demographics | Patient | — | HTML form (pre-filled or empty) |
52	| POST | `/patient/demographics` | Create/update own demographics | Patient | `full_name`, `date_of_birth`, `gender`, `phone`, `address_*`, `emergency_contact_*`, `insurance_id`, `government_id` | HTML partial (success banner) |
53	| POST | `/patient/demographics/reveal` | Reveal masked sensitive field | Patient | `field` (insurance_id/government_id) | HTML partial (unmasked value) |
54	| GET | `/staff/patients/<id>/demographics` | View patient demographics | Front Desk, Clinician | — | HTML form (editable for Front Desk, read-only for Clinician) |
55	| POST | `/staff/patients/<id>/demographics` | Edit patient demographics | Front Desk | Same fields as patient update | HTML partial (success banner) |
56	
57	### Field Validation
58	- `full_name`: required, 1-200 chars
59	- `date_of_birth`: required, ISO date, not in the future
60	- `phone`: required, US format
61	- `address_zip`: 5 or 9 digit US ZIP
62	- `insurance_id`, `government_id`: encrypted at rest, masked in display (last 4 only)
63	
64	---
65	
66	## Health Assessments (`/assessments`, `/staff/assessments`)
67	
68	| Method | Path | Description | Auth | Request | Response |
69	|--------|------|-------------|------|---------|----------|
70	| GET | `/assessments/start/<visit_id>` | Begin assessment wizard | Patient | — | HTML wizard (step 1: PHQ-9) |
71	| POST | `/assessments/save-draft` | Save partial progress | Patient | `visit_id`, `template_id`, `partial_answers` (JSON) | HTML partial (confirmation) |
72	| POST | `/assessments/submit/<visit_id>` | Finalize assessment | Patient | `answers` (JSON), `_request_token` | Redirect to result page |
73	| GET | `/assessments/result/<assessment_id>` | View result with explanation | Patient, Clinician | — | HTML page (scores, risk level, rules) |
74	| GET | `/assessments/history` | Patient's assessment history | Patient | `?page=` | HTML timeline/list |
75	| GET | `/staff/assessments/<patient_id>` | Clinician view of patient assessments | Clinician, Front Desk | — | HTML list of assessments |
76	
77	### Assessment Templates
78	- **PHQ-9**: 9 questions, 0-3 scale, total 0-27 → Minimal/Mild/Moderate/Moderately Severe/Severe
79	- **GAD-7**: 7 questions, 0-3 scale, total 0-21 → Minimal/Mild/Moderate/Severe
80	- **Blood Pressure**: Self-reported category (Normal/Elevated/Stage 1/Stage 2/Crisis)
81	- **Fall Risk**: Yes/No flags (history of falls, mobility aids, dizziness, balance medications)
82	- **Medication Adherence**: 4-question scale (never/rarely/sometimes/often miss)
83	
84	### Risk Stratification Rules
85	- **High**: PHQ-9 ≥ 15 OR GAD-7 ≥ 15 OR BP = Crisis OR fall-risk ≥ 2 flags
86	- **Moderate**: PHQ-9 10-14 OR GAD-7 10-14 OR BP = Stage 1/2 OR fall-risk = 1 flag
87	- **Low**: All scores below moderate thresholds
88	
89	---
90	
91	## Scheduling (`/schedule`, `/staff/schedule`, `/admin/schedule`)
92	
93	| Method | Path | Description | Auth | Request | Response |
94	|--------|------|-------------|------|---------|----------|
95	| GET | `/schedule/available` | Search available slots | Patient, Front Desk | `?date_from=`, `?date_to=`, `?clinician_id=` | HTML slot list |
96	| POST | `/schedule/hold/<slot_id>` | Create 10-min reservation hold | Patient, Front Desk | `_request_token` | HTML partial (countdown timer) |
97	| POST | `/schedule/confirm/<reservation_id>` | Confirm booking | Patient, Front Desk | `_request_token` | HTML partial (booking confirmation) |
98	| DELETE | `/schedule/cancel/<reservation_id>` | Cancel hold or booking | Patient, Front Desk | `_request_token`, `reason` (optional) | HTML partial (cancellation confirmation) |
99	| GET | `/staff/schedule/calendar` | Staff calendar view | Front Desk, Clinician, Admin | `?week=`, `?clinician_id=` | HTML calendar (week view) |
100	| POST | `/admin/schedule/bulk-generate` | Bulk slot generation | Admin | `clinician_id`, `date_from`, `date_to`, `template_id` | HTML partial (generated slot count) |
101	| GET | `/admin/holidays` | List holidays | Admin | — | HTML list |
102	| POST | `/admin/holidays` | Add holiday | Admin | `date`, `name` | HTML partial (updated list) |
103	| DELETE | `/admin/holidays/<id>` | Remove holiday | Admin | — | HTML partial (updated list) |
104	
105	### Slot Defaults
106	- 15-minute duration, 1 patient capacity per clinician slot
107	- Reservation hold expires after 10 minutes
108	- Maximum 2 simultaneous holds per patient
109	
110	---
111	
112	## Visits & Dashboard (`/visits`, `/dashboard`)
113	
114	| Method | Path | Description | Auth | Request | Response |
115	|--------|------|-------------|------|---------|----------|
116	| GET | `/dashboard` | Shared visit dashboard (today's visits) | Front Desk, Clinician, Admin | `?clinician_id=`, `?status=` | HTML dashboard table |
117	| POST | `/visits/<id>/transition` | Advance visit state | Front Desk, Clinician, Admin | `target_state`, `reason` (optional), `_request_token` | HTML partial (updated row) |
118	| GET | `/visits/<id>/timeline` | Milestone timeline for a visit | Front Desk, Clinician, Admin | — | HTML partial (transition history) |
119	| GET | `/dashboard/poll` | HTMX polling for dashboard updates | Front Desk, Clinician, Admin | — | HTML partial (updated rows) |
120	
121	### State Machine Transitions
122	```
123	Booked → Pending Payment → Checked In → Seen
124	Booked → Checked In → Seen
125	Booked → Canceled
126	Pending Payment → Canceled
127	Checked In → No-Show
128	Any active → Canceled (admin override, reason required)
129	```
130	
131	### Idempotency
132	- `_request_token` (UUID) generated per form load, consumed on use
133	- Duplicate token → 409 Conflict with "This action has already been processed"
134	- Optimistic concurrency: `UPDATE ... WHERE status = <expected>` prevents race conditions
135	
136	---
137	
138	## Service Coverage Zones (`/admin/zones`, `/delivery`)
139	
140	| Method | Path | Description | Auth | Request | Response |
141	|--------|------|-------------|------|---------|----------|
142	| GET | `/admin/zones` | List all coverage zones | Admin | — | HTML table |
143	| POST | `/admin/zones` | Create zone | Admin | `name`, `description`, `zip_codes[]`, `distance_band_min`, `distance_band_max`, `min_order_amount`, `delivery_fee`, `delivery_windows[]` | HTML partial (updated table) |
144	| PUT | `/admin/zones/<id>` | Update zone | Admin | Same fields as create | HTML partial (updated row) |
145	| DELETE | `/admin/zones/<id>` | Soft-delete/deactivate zone | Admin | — | HTML partial (updated row) |
146	| GET | `/delivery/check` | Check delivery eligibility | Patient, Front Desk | `?zip=<zip_code>` | HTML partial (eligible: fee, windows / not eligible message) |
147	
148	### Zone Constraints
149	- ZIP codes unique across active zones
150	- Delivery windows within a zone cannot overlap
151	- Min order and delivery fee must be ≥ $0.00
152	
153	---
154	
155	## Reminders (`/patient/reminders`, `/staff/reminders`, `/admin/reminders`)
156	
157	| Method | Path | Description | Auth | Request | Response |
158	|--------|------|-------------|------|---------|----------|
159	| GET | `/patient/reminders` | List active reminders | Patient | — | HTML partial (reminder list) |
160	| POST | `/patient/reminders/<id>/dismiss` | Dismiss a reminder | Patient | — | HTML partial (updated list) |
161	| GET | `/patient/reminders/count` | Badge count for nav | Patient | — | HTML partial (count badge) |
162	| GET | `/staff/reminders` | All pending patient reminders | Front Desk, Clinician | — | HTML table |
163	| GET | `/admin/reminders/config` | Reassessment interval config | Admin | — | HTML form |
164	| PUT | `/admin/reminders/config/<template_id>` | Update interval | Admin | `interval_days` | HTML partial (confirmation) |
165	
166	### Defaults
167	- Chronic-care reassessment: every 90 days
168	- Pre-visit reminder: 24 hours before appointment
169	
170	---
171	
172	## Audit Log (`/admin/audit`) — Admin Only
173	
174	| Method | Path | Description | Auth | Request | Response |
175	|--------|------|-------------|------|---------|----------|
176	| GET | `/admin/audit` | Audit log viewer | Admin | `?event_type=`, `?actor_id=`, `?target_type=`, `?target_id=`, `?date_from=`, `?date_to=`, `?page=` | HTML table with pagination |
177	| GET | `/admin/audit/export` | Export audit data | Admin | `?format=csv|json`, `?date_from=`, `?date_to=` | File download (CSV or JSON) |
178	| GET | `/admin/audit/entity/<type>/<id>` | Timeline for specific entity | Admin | — | HTML partial (event timeline) |
179	
180	---
181	
182	## Data Export & Deletion (`/patient`)
183	
184	| Method | Path | Description | Auth | Request | Response |
185	|--------|------|-------------|------|---------|----------|
186	| GET | `/patient/export` | Download personal data | Patient | — | JSON file download |
187	| POST | `/patient/delete-account` | Request account anonymization | Patient | `password` (re-authentication), signed timestamp | Session terminated, redirect to login |
188	
189	### Anonymization
190	- Name → `ANON-<hash>`, email/phone/IDs → null
191	- Visit dates, assessment scores preserved (de-identified)
192	- Audit events retain structure with anonymized actor
193	
194	---
195	
196	## Admin Operations (`/admin/operations`) — Admin Only
197	
198	| Method | Path | Description | Auth | Request | Response |
199	|--------|------|-------------|------|---------|----------|
200	| GET | `/admin/operations` | Operations dashboard | Admin | — | HTML dashboard |
201	| GET | `/admin/operations/alerts` | HTMX partial for alerts | Admin | — | HTML partial (alert list) |
202	| POST | `/admin/operations/alerts/<id>/acknowledge` | Acknowledge alert | Admin | — | HTML partial (updated alert) |
203	| GET | `/admin/operations/slow-queries` | HTMX partial for slow queries | Admin | — | HTML partial (query table) |
204	| GET | `/admin/operations/sessions` | Active sessions | Admin | — | HTML partial (session table) |
205	
206	---
207	
208	## Common Headers & Conventions
209	
210	### Request Headers
211	| Header | Purpose | Required |
212	|--------|---------|----------|
213	| `X-CSRFToken` | CSRF protection (HTMX requests) | All POST/PUT/DELETE |
214	| `X-Request-Token` | Idempotency token | State-changing operations |
215	| `X-Signed-Timestamp` | Anti-replay signed timestamp | Sensitive actions (login, transitions, deletion) |
216	| `X-Signed-Nonce` | Anti-replay nonce | Sensitive actions |
217	| `HX-Request` | HTMX request indicator (auto-set by HTMX) | HTMX calls |
218	
219	### Response Headers
220	| Header | Purpose |
221	|--------|---------|
222	| `X-Correlation-ID` | Request correlation UUID for tracing |
223	| `Strict-Transport-Security` | HSTS enforcement |
224	| `X-Content-Type-Options` | `nosniff` |
225	| `X-Frame-Options` | `DENY` |
226	| `Content-Security-Policy` | CSP rules |
227	
228	### Error Responses
229	| Status | Meaning | Body |
230	|--------|---------|------|
231	| 400 | Validation error | HTML partial with field errors / JSON `{"error": "...", "fields": {...}}` |
232	| 403 | Forbidden (CSRF fail, auth fail, role denied) | HTML 403 page / JSON `{"error": "Access denied"}` |
233	| 404 | Not found | HTML 404 page / JSON `{"error": "Not found"}` |
234	| 409 | Conflict (duplicate token, stale state) | HTML message / JSON `{"error": "Already processed"}` |
235	| 429 | Rate limited | HTML message with retry-after / JSON `{"error": "Too many attempts", "retry_after_seconds": N}` |
236

1	import hashlib
2	from datetime import datetime, timezone
3	from functools import wraps
4	from flask import request, jsonify
5	from flask_login import current_user
6	from app.extensions import db
7	from app.models.idempotency import RequestToken
8	
9	
10	def _hash_token(token: str) -> str:
11	    """One-way hash an idempotency token for safe at-rest storage."""
12	    return hashlib.sha256(token.encode()).hexdigest()
13	
14	
15	# Public alias used by routes that persist their own token columns.
16	hash_token = _hash_token
17	
18	
19	def check_idempotency(token, user_id=None):
20	    """Return cached result dict if token exists and hasn't expired, else None."""
21	    if not token:
22	        return None
23	    record = RequestToken.query.filter_by(token=_hash_token(token)).first()
24	    if record and user_id is not None and record.user_id is not None and record.user_id != user_id:
25	        return None
26	    if record is None:
27	        return None
28	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
29	    expires = record.expires_at
30	    if expires:
31	        if expires.tzinfo is None:
32	            expires = expires.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
33	        if now > expires:
34	            db.session.delete(record)
35	            db.session.commit()
36	            return None
37	    return record.result_json
38	
39	
40	def save_idempotency(token, endpoint, result=None, user_id=None):
41	    """Save a result for the given idempotency token (stored as hash)."""
42	    if not token:
43	        return
44	    token_hash = _hash_token(token)
45	    record = RequestToken.query.filter_by(token=token_hash).first()
46	    if record:
47	        return  # already saved
48	    record = RequestToken(
49	        token=token_hash,
50	        endpoint=endpoint,
51	        result_json=result,
52	        user_id=user_id,
53	    )
54	    db.session.add(record)
55	    db.session.commit()
56	    return record
57	
58	
59	def idempotent(f):
60	    @wraps(f)
61	    def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
62	        token = request.form.get("_request_token") or request.headers.get("X-Request-Token")
63	        if token:
64	            user_id = current_user.id if current_user.is_authenticated else None
65	            existing = check_idempotency(token, user_id=user_id)
66	            if existing:
67	                return jsonify({"error": "This action has already been processed"}), 409
68	        result = f(*args, **kwargs)
69	        if token:
70	            user_id = current_user.id if current_user.is_authenticated else None
71	            save_idempotency(token, request.path, user_id=user_id)
72	        return result
73	    return decorated
74

105	def expire_stale_holds():
106	    """Expire held reservations past their expiry time."""
107	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
108	    stale = Reservation.query.filter(
109	        Reservation.status == "held",
110	        Reservation.expires_at <= now,
111	    ).all()
112	    for res in stale:
113	        res.status = "expired"
114	    if stale:
115	        db.session.commit()
116	    return len(stale)

1	import hashlib
2	from datetime import datetime, timezone
3	from app.extensions import db
4	from app.models.visit import Visit, VisitTransition
5	
6	
7	def _hash_token(token: str) -> str:
8	    """One-way hash a request token for safe at-rest storage."""
9	    return hashlib.sha256(token.encode()).hexdigest()
10	
11	VALID_TRANSITIONS = {
12	    "booked": {"pending_payment", "checked_in", "canceled", "no_show"},
13	    "pending_payment": {"checked_in", "canceled"},
14	    "checked_in": {"seen", "canceled", "no_show"},
15	    "seen": set(),
16	    "canceled": set(),
17	    "no_show": set(),
18	}
19	
20	TERMINAL_STATES = {"seen", "canceled", "no_show"}
21	
22	
23	def transition_visit(visit, to_status, changed_by_id, reason=None, request_token=None):
24	    """Transition a visit to a new status with optimistic concurrency control.
25	
26	    Raises ValueError for invalid transitions.
27	    Returns the VisitTransition record on success.
28	    """
29	    if to_status not in Visit.VALID_STATUSES:
30	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid target status: {to_status}")
31	
32	    from_status = visit.status
33	
34	    if from_status in TERMINAL_STATES:
35	        raise ValueError(f"Cannot transition from terminal state: {from_status}")
36	
37	    allowed = VALID_TRANSITIONS.get(from_status, set())
38	    if to_status not in allowed:
39	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid transition from '{from_status}' to '{to_status}'")
40	
41	    # Check for duplicate request token (stored/looked up as SHA-256 hash)
42	    token_hash = _hash_token(request_token) if request_token else None
43	    if token_hash:
44	        existing = VisitTransition.query.filter_by(request_token=token_hash).first()
45	        if existing:
46	            return existing
47	
48	    # Optimistic concurrency: re-check current state
49	    current = db.session.get(Visit, visit.id)
50	    if current.status != from_status:
51	        raise ValueError(f"Visit status changed concurrently (expected '{from_status}', got '{current.status}')")
52	
53	    transition = VisitTransition(
54	        visit_id=visit.id,
55	        from_status=from_status,
56	        to_status=to_status,
57	        changed_by=changed_by_id,
58	        reason=reason,
59	        request_token=token_hash,
60	    )
61	    db.session.add(transition)
62	    visit.status = to_status
63	    visit.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
64	    db.session.commit()
65	
66	    return transition
67

1	import json
2	from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
3	from flask import request, has_request_context
4	from flask_login import current_user
5	from app.extensions import db
6	from app.models.audit import AuditLog, AnomalyAlert
7	from app.models.user import LoginAttempt
8	
9	_NEW_DEVICE_COOLDOWN = timedelta(hours=24)
10	
11	
12	def log_action(action, resource_type, resource_id=None, details=None):
13	    """Log an audit event capturing the current user and request context."""
14	    user_id = None
15	    ip_address = None
16	    user_agent = None
17	
18	    if has_request_context():
19	        if current_user and hasattr(current_user, "id") and current_user.is_authenticated:
20	            user_id = current_user.id
21	        ip_address = request.headers.get("X-Forwarded-For", request.remote_addr) or "unknown"
22	        user_agent = (request.headers.get("User-Agent", "") or "")[:500]
23	
24	    entry = AuditLog(
25	        user_id=user_id,
26	        action=action,
27	        resource_type=resource_type,
28	        resource_id=str(resource_id) if resource_id is not None else None,
29	        details_json=details,
30	        ip_address=ip_address,
31	        user_agent=user_agent,
32	    )
33	    db.session.add(entry)
34	    db.session.commit()
35	    return entry
36	
37	
38	def check_new_device_alert(user_id, username, ip, ua):
39	    """Create AnomalyAlert when a user logs in from a new IP or new device.
40	
41	    A new IP/device is one not seen in any prior successful login for this user.
42	    Alerts are deduplicated within a 24-hour cooldown window per user+IP and
43	    user+device to prevent alert fatigue.  Called after _record_attempt so the
44	    current login is already persisted — we exclude the most-recent record.
45	    """
46	    try:
47	        # All successful logins for this username, oldest-first.
48	        # The last entry is the one just recorded (current login).
49	        all_ok = (
50	            LoginAttempt.query
51	            .filter_by(username=username, success=True)
52	            .order_by(LoginAttempt.attempted_at.asc())
53	            .all()
54	        )
55	        # If this is the very first login there are no prior sessions to compare.
56	        if len(all_ok) <= 1:
57	            return
58	
59	        prior = all_ok[:-1]  # everything before the current login
60	        known_ips = {a.ip_address for a in prior if a.ip_address}
61	        known_uas = {a.user_agent for a in prior if a.user_agent}
62	
63	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
64	        cooldown_start = now - _NEW_DEVICE_COOLDOWN
65	
66	        # ── New IP ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
67	        if ip and ip not in known_ips:
68	            already_alerted = AnomalyAlert.query.filter(
69	                AnomalyAlert.alert_type == "new_ip",
70	                AnomalyAlert.created_at >= cooldown_start,
71	                AnomalyAlert.message.contains(f"user={username}"),
72	                AnomalyAlert.message.contains(f"ip={ip}"),
73	            ).first()
74	            if not already_alerted:
75	                db.session.add(AnomalyAlert(
76	                    alert_type="new_ip",
77	                    severity="warning",
78	                    message=f"New IP login: user={username}, ip={ip}",
79	                    details_json=json.dumps({
80	                        "user_id": user_id, "username": username, "ip": ip,
81	                    }),
82	                ))
83	
84	        # ── New device (user-agent) ──────────────────────────────────────────
85	        if ua and ua not in known_uas:
86	            already_alerted = AnomalyAlert.query.filter(
87	                AnomalyAlert.alert_type == "new_device",
88	                AnomalyAlert.created_at >= cooldown_start,
89	                AnomalyAlert.message.contains(f"user={username}"),
90	            ).first()
91	            if not already_alerted:
92	                ua_short = ua[:120]
93	                db.session.add(AnomalyAlert(
94	                    alert_type="new_device",
95	                    severity="info",
96	                    message=f"New device login: user={username}, ua={ua_short}",
97	                    details_json=json.dumps({
98	                        "user_id": user_id, "username": username, "user_agent": ua[:200],
99	                    }),
100	                ))
101	
102	        db.session.commit()
103	    except Exception:
104	        db.session.rollback()
105	
106	
107	def anomaly_detection():
108	    """Check for anomalous patterns and create alerts.
109	
110	    Currently checks:
111	    - More than 5 failed login attempts in the last 10 minutes.
112	    """
113	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
114	    window = now - timedelta(minutes=10)
115	
116	    try:
117	        failed_count = LoginAttempt.query.filter(
118	            LoginAttempt.success == False,
119	            LoginAttempt.attempted_at >= window,
120	        ).count()
121	    except Exception:
122	        failed_count = 0
123	
124	    if failed_count > 5:
125	        # Check if we already created an alert for this in the last 10 minutes
126	        recent_alert = AnomalyAlert.query.filter(
127	            AnomalyAlert.alert_type == "failed_logins",
128	            AnomalyAlert.created_at >= window,
129	        ).first()
130	        if not recent_alert:
131	            alert = AnomalyAlert(
132	                alert_type="failed_logins",
133	                severity="warning",
134	                message=f"{failed_count} failed login attempts in the last 10 minutes",
135	            )
136	            db.session.add(alert)
137	            db.session.commit()
138
